sanskrit: एतैर्विमुक्तः कौन्तेय तमोद्वारैस्त्रिभिर्नरः । आचरत्यात्मनः श्रेयस्ततो याति परां गतिम् ॥
hindiTranslation: हे कुन्तीनन्दन  इन नरकके तीनों दरवाजोंसे रहित हुआ जो मनुष्य अपने कल्याणका आचरण करता है, वह परमगतिको प्राप्त हो जाता है ॥
englishTranslation: Freed from these three gates of darkness, a person acts for their own good and reaches the highest state.
essence: Freedom from the three dark impulses opens the way to your highest good.
speaker: Krishna
simpleMeaning: When a person is free from desire, anger, and greed, they live in a way that supports their highest good and reach the highest state.
simpleInsight: Freedom is not emptiness; it gives you room to act in your own best interest.
detailedMeaning: On the battlefield, Krishna continues his warning to Arjuna after naming desire, anger, and greed as the three gates of ruin. He now shows the other side: a person who escapes these forces can live in a way that leads upward, not downward.
modernRelevance: You pause before sending the cruel text, buying the thing you do not need, or lashing out in pride. That pause can save your character.
translationLiteral: एतैः (from these) / विमुक्तः (freed) / कौन्तेय (O son of Kunti) / तैः (by those) / त्रिभिः (three) / नरः (person) / आचरति (practices) / आत्मनः (of one's own) / श्रेयः (well-being) / ततः (then) / याति (goes) / पराम् (highest) / गतिम् (state)
insight: It shifts attention from impulse to self-protective action, showing that restraint opens the way to better choices.

quoteVariants.text: Free yourself from desire, anger, and greed. Then you act for your own highest good.
Drop desire, anger, greed. Act for your highest good.
Clear the three gates, and your next step serves you.
Clear the gates. Choose your highest good.
